entries.statement: Ability to design and develop secure applications using secure development methodologies, tools, and frameworks.
Ability to design enterprise security and privacy architectures that encompass network infrastructure, system integration, and secure interfaces between information and physical systems.
Ability to develop comprehensive privacy management frameworks, policies, and compliance procedures that address organizational privacy obligations and regulatory requirements.
Ability to assess, evaluate, and monitor compliance with organizational and regulatory privacy requirements across systems, services, and business agreements.
Ability to integrate privacy and security considerations throughout organizational programs, policies, procedures, and project lifecycles.
Ability to determine data requirements and design secure data storage, integration, and management systems aligned with organizational security and privacy objectives.
Ability to conduct security design evaluations and information security risk assessments for systems, applications, and operational environments.
Ability to communicate security and privacy architectures, IT security value, and assessment findings to technical and executive audiences at appropriate levels of detail.
Ability to assess privacy risks in AI systems, document AI risk assessments, and communicate AI security implications to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to assess and manage privacy and security risks from third-party AI systems, vendor dependencies, and supply chain components.
Ability to design data backup and recovery capabilities integrated into system architecture and cloud infrastructure solutions.
Ability to design and execute secure test plans and testing protocols for security validation and compliance verification.
Ability to assess and ensure data quality, input validation, and representativeness in systems and AI applications.
Ability to monitor systems and cloud infrastructure for security and privacy compliance through continuous oversight mechanisms.
Ability to resolve privacy and security conflicts, collection gaps, and competing security requirements through evidence-based decision-making.
